codeReview.summary: The feature adds two focused regressions that explicitly prove VAL-CROSS-002: runtime-level sibling nested override contexts decrypt only their own leaves, and store-level persisted nested leaves remain encrypted at rest and fail wrong-context decryption. The implementation stays scoped to test coverage and matches the feature contract without introducing behavior changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still says GitNexus impact analysis is required before editing symbols, but this worker session completed via direct source inspection/diff review without any GitNexus call and still marked followedProcedure=true. The fallback is mission-acceptable when tooling is unavailable, but the active guidance is inconsistent about whether explicit fallback reporting is mandatory.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md require gitnexus_impact before edits; rust-library-worker SKILL.md step 3 allows fallback when GitNexus tooling is unavailable. The worker transcript for session 985ac86a-5b7a-464a-a933-69ca3204d99a shows no gitnexus command/tool use before editing and the handoff still reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Pass: commit 565419e8cafe39191797eb7a9cf980d7a59058d0 adds explicit runtime and store proofs for nested sibling field-account override isolation, and I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues.
